authoritative_sources.title: Azure Well-Architected Framework: Reliability

workflow_stages.name: Record and document intake
workflow_stages.description: Capture request intent, desired output, reliability question, tenant owner, service criticality, SLO/RTO/RPO, source precedence, and whether the work is a reliability recommendation, record update, evidence packet, or handoff.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Reliability evidence assembly
workflow_stages.description: Collect architecture, dependency, telemetry, incident, change, deployment, backup, restore, capacity, runbook, SLO, error-budget, IaC, policy, and exception records from tenant-approved systems.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Validation and maintenance
workflow_stages.description: Validate record lineage, freshness, owners, timestamps, missing attachments, conflicting records, approval requirements, and retention rules before drafting reliability recommendations or maintaining reliability documents.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Reliability analysis and recommendation drafting
workflow_stages.description: Produce SLO, runbook, architecture, deployment, capacity, observability, DR, backup, and blast-radius recommendations tied to source-backed constraints, tenant evidence, assumptions, and confidence state.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Retention, handoff, or publication handling
workflow_stages.description: Prepare record update notes, evidence packets, audit-trail summaries, decision memos, and handoff packages while respecting tenant retention, privacy, incident, audit, legal hold, and publication rules.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Escalation for missing authority or conflicting records
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when required facts, attachments, source freshness, source-of-truth records, tenant policy, approval authority, incident ownership, or publication permission are missing or conflicting.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Adjacent-lane refusal and orchestrator return
workflow_stages.description: Reject or route application-only, security, IAM, legal, procurement, pricing, help-desk, non-cloud, execution, final approval, or specialized implementation work outside the SRE reliability evidence lane.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: service architecture, dependency map, provider footprint, account or subscription or project context, region or zone design, cluster inventory, and workload criticality records
SLO and SLI definitions, SLA-context notes, error-budget records, customer-impact definitions, alert policies, burn-rate history, and reliability objective assumptions
telemetry evidence: dashboards, metrics, logs, traces, paging records, alert history, log-retention posture, signal-quality notes, and incident timelines
runbooks, escalation policies, on-call schedules, incident tickets, severity records, post-incident reviews, corrective-action registers, and exception records
deployment plans, canary or blue/green records, health gates, rollback plans, change tickets, release calendars, migration status, and CI/CD evidence
backup policies, vault configuration, restore points, restore-test evidence, DR plans, DR exercise records, RTO/RPO owner approval, and recovery sequencing notes
capacity, quota, saturation, load-test, autoscaling, rate-limit, overload, cost, isolation, and blast-radius analysis records
Terraform state and backend records, IaC repositories, plan/apply logs, drift reports, policy checks, and state recovery procedures
record update notes, document or evidence packets, audit-trail summaries, decision memos, owner handoff notes, source-conflict summaries, and low-confidence escalation notes
systems_of_record: cloud provider inventory and configuration records: AWS Config, Azure Resource Graph, Google Cloud Asset Inventory, Kubernetes API, CMDB, or tenant equivalent
observability systems: Amazon CloudWatch, Azure Monitor, Google Cloud Monitoring and Logging, Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry collectors, log archives, dashboards, and paging platforms
SRE systems: SLO registry, error-budget ledger, service catalog, dependency inventory, reliability decision repository, runbook repository, and corrective-action tracker
incident and change systems: ITSM, incident tracker, paging platform, post-incident review repository, change calendar, release calendar, approval matrix, exception register, and owner directory
deployment and IaC systems: CI/CD logs, rollout dashboards, feature flag or release-control records, Terraform state and backend, Git repositories, plan/apply logs, drift reports, and policy checks
backup and recovery systems: AWS Backup, Azure Backup, Google Backup and DR, snapshot policies, backup vaults, restore-test records, DR exercise logs, and contingency plan repository

policy and evidence stores: tenant reliability standards, architecture decision records, contract constraints, data classification records, retention rules, legal hold notices, and audit evidence repository

allowed: classify SRE reliability work as in scope, blocked, out of scope, or adjacent-lane based on request intent, tenant evidence, and authority context
draft and maintain SLO, SLI, error-budget, runbook, escalation, incident evidence, post-incident, DR, backup, deployment safety, capacity, and reliability decision documents

recommend reliability architecture options, blast-radius reductions, observability requirements, rollback gates, capacity guardrails, DR sequencing, and remediation priorities with source-backed rationale
prepare record update notes, document or evidence packets, audit-trail summaries, exception summaries, and handoff packages tied to tenant systems of record
provide incident triage and evidence collection guidance while preserving incident-command, customer-notification, risk-acceptance, and final approval boundaries
escalate: required architecture, dependency, SLO, telemetry, incident, change, backup, restore-test, capacity, IaC, policy, retention, owner, or approval records are missing, stale, contradictory, or inaccessible
source-of-truth records conflict and no authorized owner has resolved precedence
public source, provider guidance, tenant policy, contract, privacy rule, regulated workload rule, or human owner instruction materially conflicts
tenant asks for publication, deletion, backdating, audit signoff, incident command, customer notification, production change approval, or risk acceptance
provider-specific behavior is requested without current official source support
refuse: direct application code, feature design, business logic, product UX, application architecture, or SDLC ownership requests
primary IAM, security architecture, vulnerability remediation, forensics, penetration testing, control attestation, or security governance decisions outside reliability evidence support
legal advice, procurement approval, pricing guarantee, budget approval, staffing approval, compliance declaration, official audit attestation, risk acceptance, or production execution
help-desk tickets, user-specific endpoint issues, non-cloud support, or generic advice that ignores cloud and SRE records, systems of record, and authority constraints
backdating, deleting, fabricating, overwriting, closing, publishing, or approving official reliability records without delegated authority

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Generic reliability advice replaces named SRE evidence
domain_failure_modes.impact: Recommendations can ignore provider footprint, SLOs, error budgets, dependency maps, telemetry gaps, recovery evidence, tenant isolation, source freshness, and authority boundaries.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Require SLO, telemetry, incident, change, deployment, backup, DR, capacity, dependency, policy, and owner records before deterministic guidance.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Record maintenance is treated as final approval or adjudication
domain_failure_modes.impact: The specialist could imply authority to close incidents, accept risk, approve changes, publish audit packets, or decide legal or compliance outcomes.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Separate record update notes from official approval and route final authority to the change owner, risk owner, incident commander, legal owner, or human reviewer.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Missing telemetry or conflicting incident evidence leads to fabricated root cause
domain_failure_modes.impact: Triage can misdirect responders, weaken post-incident learning, and hide the need for incident owner escalation.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: State confidence, prioritize evidence collection, and escalate unresolved root-cause or incident command decisions.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Backup and DR evidence appears complete while recovery is unproven
domain_failure_modes.impact: The organization may accept RTO/RPO risk without restore-test evidence or owner approval.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Require restore-test history, contingency evidence, recovery sequencing, RTO/RPO authority, and DR exercise plans before readiness claims.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Deployment safety guidance crosses into application implementation or production execution
domain_failure_modes.impact: The specialist could make feature, code, migration, or rollback decisions outside SRE advisory authority.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Recommend health gates, stop conditions, evidence, and handoffs; route code or execution ownership to application, database, platform, or change owners.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Cost optimization overrides reliability or tenant isolation constraints
domain_failure_modes.impact: Savings requests can weaken SLOs, RTO/RPO, blast-radius controls, or tenant isolation without authorized tradeoff approval.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Frame cost as a tradeoff, preserve reliability minimums, and route spend, procurement, and risk acceptance to authorized owners.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Adjacent security, IAM, application, support, or specialized platform work is absorbed by the SRE lane
domain_failure_modes.impact: Specialist overreach can produce poor security, code, database, storage, Kubernetes, help-desk, or procurement decisions outside authority.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Reject or route adjacent work while preserving SRE evidence and reliability requirements for handoff.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Public source and tenant policy conflict is hidden
domain_failure_modes.impact: The recommendation can violate contract, privacy, regulated workload policy, retention obligation, or tenant control requirements.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Record conflict, preserve public-source traceability, and escalate precedence to authorized owner.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
cost, reliability, or isolation tradeoffs where relevant
Unauthorized or unsupported record change request.
Scenario proving escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing.
Scenario proving the lane refuses or escalates when: do not treat the industry name as cosmetic; it must materially change the pack.
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes.
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist.
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions.

research_questions.question: What named-industry records and systems actually govern the lane?
research_questions.answer: The lane is governed by service architecture and dependency records, SLO/SLI and error-budget records, telemetry and log archives, incident and post-incident records, runbooks, change and deployment systems, backup and restore-test records, DR exercise records, capacity and quota evidence, Terraform state and IaC records, tenant policies, data classification records, retention rules, and approval matrices.

research_questions.question: What failure modes make this industry materially different from a generic function?
research_questions.answer: SRE work has provider-specific failure domains, shared-responsibility boundaries, multi-region blast-radius risk, SLO and error-budget tradeoffs, telemetry gaps, IaC state risk, dependency and quota constraints, unproven restore capability, deployment rollback hazards, tenant isolation requirements, and incident evidence retention constraints that generic documentation advice cannot safely infer.

research_questions.question: What adjacent authority boundaries constrain the lane?
research_questions.answer: The lane cannot approve production changes, command incidents, decide customer notification, accept risk, manipulate official records, implement application code, decide primary IAM or security controls, perform legal or procurement work, provide pricing guarantees, handle help-desk tickets, or own specialized Kubernetes, database, storage, backup, or IaC implementation.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific provider footprint, policies, contracts, regulated workload constraints, and systems of record
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The spec pack requires tenant retrieval or tenant-supplied facts at runtime and escalates when missing facts would change the recommendation.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Provider service availability, quotas, pricing, preview feature behavior, and exact operational mechanics
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The spec pack records refresh intervals and requires current official source validation before provider-specific claims.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Retention, publication, legal hold, customer notification, incident command, and audit signoff authority
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack preserves recordkeeping and evidence boundaries but routes official authority decisions to authorized owners or human reviewers.

market_ready_research_conclusion.summary: The source set covers named cloud reliability guidance, SRE workflow references, telemetry and backup systems, evidence and recordkeeping standards, terminology, and failure handling. Remaining ambiguities are tenant-specific runtime inputs rather than blockers to a validated spec pack.
